title: making a homemade low-cost agarose gel electrophoresis tank apparatus
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"73k1b","text":"this guide gives a brief and simple method to make an agarose gel electrophoresis tank from house materials, it take nearly 2 hours to make and costs only 5-10$","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anrg4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: to make the tray for the aragorse gel electrophoresis tank, you need to take a rectangular peace of plexiglass you can choose the dimensions you need.
and draw with a marker a straight line of 2 or 3 cm on each vertical side.

put the angle 5-10 cm away from a fire stove for 20 or 30 seconds to soften the plexiglass and bend the angle according to the line you drew. (make sure you continue moving the plexiglass or it will melt, and be careful not to burn your hands and use a glove or a clamp)
use the angle between the ground and ceiling to create a straight angle from the plexiglass, and make sure you press the plexiglass with a solid object to make the angle with best form.
repeat the same process for the next side.
for the combs you can use plexiglass and any laser cutter store can do it, you can choose the number of wells you desire. the shape of the tray that should look like
make a groove using a wood file or a small saw in order to fix the comb on it the groove shape on the tray
the apparatus is made by using two wood plaques, bigger than the tray then the wood is drilled with a distance bigger 2 cm of the final edge of the tray.
enter two tall screws with bolts in inside the wooden plaque through the holes.
fix the tall screws using bolts, and use butterfly bolts to tighten the wood plaques on the tray
to create a seal, use two plastic sponge or foam and glue it on the wooden plaques (a new plastics from floor wiper will workout fine).
the final shape of the tray with removable seal should look like this
for the tank, you can use a large plastic tupperware, enought for the tray to fit in and the buffer to cover the tray 3 cm above it.
after you choose the tank, you can put a 0.5 mm wire made from stainless steel and fix it on both end of the tank, and let the wire touch the lowest floor of the tank.
use electrical clips connected to copper wires to connect the tank to the electrical powerbox
the shape of the tank should look like this

to operate the tank use the wooden clamps and pour the agarose gel with the combs wait for the gel to cool and remove combs put the tray in the tank filled with TAE or TBE buffer then load samples and connect the wires and start the electrophoresis
creator: Joseph shenekji
